channel.title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
channel.description: Breaking news and world news from France 24 on Business, Sports, Culture. Video news. News from the US, Europe, Asia Pacific, Africa, Middle East, America

item.title: Millions gather in Mecca for Hajj despite security concerns
item.description: Despite security concerns linked to the conflict in the Middle East, pilgrims from around the world continue to arrive in Mecca for this year’s Hajj. Saudi officials say more than 1.5 million people have already reached the kingdom, with numbers expected to rise further as many say they are willing to take the risk to complete the pilgrimage.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Middle East: Deep mistrust clouds US-Iran negotiations
item.description: Rouzbeh Parsi, Adjunct Senior Lecturer at Lund University, is FRANCE 24’s guest to discuss the evolving situation in the Middle East. Iran and the United States are both seeking a possible diplomatic way out of the conflict, but uncertainty remains high amid conflicting signals from both sides. Parsi highlights the contradictions in the current negotiations and the difficulty in finding a credible agreement, particularly as mistrust continues to dominate relations. While the risk of escalation into a wider war remains, both Washington and Tehran appear to be looking for an exit strategy from prolonged confrontation.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Uncertainty persists over US-Iran draft agreement
item.description: Iran and the United States say progress has been made in talks over a 14-point memorandum of understanding, but both sides insist a final deal is not imminent. While US officials, including Secretary of State Marco Rubio, describe the process as a “work in progress,” Tehran has accused Washington of sending mixed messages and says key details are still unresolved.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: France swelters as first-ever May heatwave alert issued
item.description: France experienced record high temperatures for May over the weekend, according to the country’s national weather service, with Paris recording this year's first temperature above 30C on Saturday. A spokesperson for Meteo France added that a yellow heatwave alert issued Sunday was the very first “to be issued in May since the system was created in 2004".
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Yamal headlines Spain squad as Real Madrid stars miss World Cup cut
item.description: Spain coach Luis de la Fuente named teenage sensation Lamine Yamal in his 2026 World Cup squad on Monday, while recalling Mikel Merino and Gavi after injury layoffs and excluding Real Madrid players for the first time in the nation’s World Cup history.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Ebola outbreak raises fears of wider spread in Central Africa
item.description: An Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda is raising fears of wider spread, with over 900 suspected cases and more than 200 deaths reported in the DRC. Health workers warn the toll is likely higher due to limited testing, while conflict, mistrust, and lack of treatment for the rare strain are hampering containment efforts.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Runners collapse as temperatures soar across France
item.description: An unusually early heatwave has pushed temperatures above 30°C across France, leading to multiple medical emergencies during running events. In suburbs of Paris and other cities, dozens of participants collapsed, with several hospitalised in serious condition and emergency services overwhelmed by heat-related incidents.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: 'Self-destructive' Putin loses home support as Ukraine war rages on
item.description: PRESS REVIEW – Monday, 25 May 2026: First, papers discuss whether Russian President Vladimir Putin has lost support from his backers. Next, a police raid of opposition party headquarters in Turkey is getting considerable coverage. Also, the Enhanced Games do not lead to the many breakthroughs that were expected, with only one new world "record". Meanwhile, a 99-year-old breaks records in the sky. Finally, actors called Chris and talking animals are more likely to make it in Hollywood than women over the age of 60.
item.dc:creator: Emily BOYLE

item.title: India’s “Cockroach Janta Party” goes viral as Gen Z movement grows
item.description: A viral online joke in India, the “Cockroach Janta Party,” created as a parody of comments by a Supreme Court judge, has grown into a Gen Z–driven political movement with millions of followers. It promotes youth frustration with traditional politics but has also faced attempts by authorities to restrict its spread and reports of threats against its founder.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Security and social reform dominate Colombia’s presidential race
item.description: Colombia is heading into a high-stakes presidential election on Sunday, amid rising violence, inflation, and deep political division. Voters will choose between continuing left-wing social reforms under Ivan Cepeda, a centrist-right shift led by Paloma Valencia, or a hardline security agenda from Abelardo De La Espriella, with a possible runoff if no candidate wins outright.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: France: Air conditioning demand surges during early heatwave
item.description: More French households are turning to air conditioning as heatwaves become more frequent, despite traditionally low adoption rates compared with countries like the United States or Japan. Rising temperatures are driving demand, but many people remain concerned about the environmental impact and the added cost of cooling their homes.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Philippines: Deadly building collapse traps workers in Manila
item.description: A nine-storey building under construction collapsed in the Philippine capital Manila, killing several people and leaving around a dozen missing, most of them construction workers trapped under the rubble. Rescue operations remain difficult because of the unstable structure, while authorities revealed the project had previously been suspended over safety violations before work resumed last year.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: 'Work in progress': Washington seeks diplomatic exit from Iran war
item.description: Despite signs of progress, uncertainty still surrounds a possible agreement between Washington and Tehran, with both sides signalling caution over the terms of any final deal. US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said on Sunday that negotiations were still a “work in progress”, though his comments suggested growing confidence that a breakthrough could eventually be reached. FRANCE 24's Douglas Herbert has more.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Oil prices ease on renewed hopes of US-Iran peace deal
item.description: Oil futures slid on Monday as traders assessed US President Donald Trump's announcement over the weekend that a deal with Iran had been "largely negotiated". The benchmark Brent crude still remains much higher than when the war started on February 28. Also in this edition: some commercial vessels are managing to cross the Strait of Hormuz. Plus, Ferrari is set to launch its first fully electric supercar model.
item.dc:creator: Charles PELLEGRIN

item.title: The mullet makes a comeback at European championship
item.description: The mullet hairstyle is enjoying a revival, with around 2,000 fans gathering in Belgium for the European Mullet Championship. Beyond the haircut itself, participants described the mullet as a symbol of freedom, individuality and resistance to conformity.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Turkish police storm CHP headquarters amid political crisis
item.description: Turkish police stormed the headquarters of the main opposition CHP party in Ankara, using tear gas and rubber bullets to remove leader Ozgur Ozel after a court annulled his 2023 election victory. Critics and protesters denounced the move as politically motivated and accused President Recep Tayyip Erdogan of intensifying pressure on the opposition.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Fresh strikes test fragile Israel-Hezbollah truce
item.description: Israeli strikes killed two people in southern Lebanon during the weekend despite the ongoing ceasefire with Hezbollah. At the same time, Hezbollah leader Naim Qassem rejected talks with Israel, opposed disarmament, and called for protests against the Lebanese government, drawing condemnation from the United States.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Donald Trump slows momentum towards an Iran agreement
item.description: US President Donald Trump has tempered expectations of a swift agreement with Iran despite signs of progress in negotiations. The talks reportedly focus on Iran pledging not to acquire nuclear weapons in exchange for possible sanctions relief and an easing of US economic pressure.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Anti-Semitism was 'left unchecked’ in Australia after Gaza war, spy chief says
item.description: Australia’s spy chief on Monday told an inquiry into the Bondi Beach mass shooting that anti-Semitism was left unchecked in the country after the start of the war in Gaza. Australian Security Intelligence Organisation head Mike Burgess added that before the attack that killed 15 people attending a Jewish Hanukkah celebration in December 2025, anti-Semitism had escalated in severity from “intimidating behaviour to direct targeting of people”.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Premier League: Tottenham beat Everton to stay in top flight
item.description: Tottenham beat Everton on the final day of the Premier League season and secured their place in the league. Chelsea will miss out on all European competitions.
item.dc:creator: L'Equipe TV

item.title: Middle East war live: Iran says agreement with US ‘not imminent’ despite progress
item.description: Iran's foreign ministry spokesman Esmaeil Baqaei warned on Monday that an agreement with the US to end the war was “not imminent”, despite Tehran and Washington making progress "on a large portion of the issues" in their exchanges. US Secretary of State Marco Rubio earlier said that a deal could materialise "today". Follow our liveblog for all the latest updates.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Pope Leo XIV calls for 'robust' regulation of AI in sweeping manifesto
item.description: Pope Leo XIV on Monday published his long-awaited manifesto on artificial intelligence outlining the Catholic Church’s response to the technology’s ethical and social challenges. The US-born pontiff presented the eagerly awaited “Magnifica Humanitas” encyclical at the Vatican alongside senior Church officials and leading AI experts after having announced upon his election that he considered AI to be the biggest challenge facing humanity today.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Hajj pilgrims battle Mecca's scorching heat with ice cream and giant fans
item.description: More than a million worshippers gathered in Mecca on Monday for the Hajj, the annual pilgrimage to Islam's holiest city, but the harsh heat has been brutal for many attendees. This week’s daytime temperatures will hover between 42 and 47 degrees Celsius, according to Saudi Arabian officials, and some pilgrims have already experienced heatstroke, fainting spells and even cardiac arrest.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: 'It's blue!': Scientists discover new deep-sea octopus near Galapagos Islands
item.description: Scientists have discovered a new species of octopus nearly 1,800 metres below the water's surface near the Galapagos Islands. The team from the Charles Darwin Foundation named the “tiny” cerulean cephalopod Microeledone galapagensis because of its blue hue, which is believed to be the rarest colour in nature.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24
